speaker: narrator
text: Just because the finals had started, didn’t mean anything had changed.

speaker: narrator
text: Was it because the atmosphere had already been a little more serious, and the expectations could only be a little higher?

speaker: narrator
text: The seats of the sect leaders were still up on the podium.

speaker: narrator
text: The only thing that had changed, albeit minutely, was the layout.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘The Nine Great Sects One Union.

speaker: narrator
text: And it was the same for the Five Great Families.’ Hyun Jong glanced down at his chair.

speaker: narrator
text: No, he confirmed that itwashis chair.

speaker: narrator
text: Everyone had been shifted, and now his seat wasn’t in the second row… it was now in the frontmost row.

speaker: narrator
text: His chair was added right next to the seats of the Nine Great Sects and Five Great Families.

speaker: narrator
text: -The seating arrangements really don’t matter, but I am a little embarrassed because the sect leader with the most disciples in the finals is made to sit in the second row.

speaker: narrator
text: The words of Tang Gunak had made a decisive blow.

speaker: narrator
text: In the end, the sect leaders who had sensitive ears simply agreed and moved.

speaker: narrator
text: AlthoUgh this was only an evaluation based on their performance, Mount Hua was recognized as good enoUgh to stand shoulder-to-shoulder with the other sects.

speaker: narrator
text: If things had been normal, blinking at the man was the only consideration they needed to show.

speaker: narrator
text: But only if things were normal.

speaker: narrator
text: Unfortunately, even the current Hyun Jong wasn’t normal.

speaker: narrator
text: The sounds of the other sect leaders gossiping could be heard around him.

speaker: hyun jong
text: “People are gambling in the holy land of Shaolin, what the hell?”

speaker: hyun jong
text: “What sect are they from?”

speaker: hyun jong
text: “I heard he is the gate leader of the Huayoung Gate.”

speaker: hyun jong
text: “Huayoung Gate? Has anyone ever heard of them before?”

speaker: narrator
text: Whenever Huayoung Gate and gambling came up, Hyun Jong flinched as if someone had poked him in the ass.

speaker: narrator
text: As he had thoUght, it might be better to run away right now.

speaker: narrator
text: But since he had already climbed up the podium, he couldn’t do it.

speaker: narrator
text: At that moment, Wudang Sect’s Heo Do Jinin smiled subtly and opened his mouth, In a slightly low voice, he mumbled,

speaker: hyun jong
text: “It seems like a place I know?”

speaker: narrator
text: Hyun Jong’s face paled as he looked at him.

speaker: narrator
text: In the past, Mount Hua had collided with Wudang for Huayoung.

speaker: narrator
text: Heo Do Jinin, who was from Wudang, was bound to know that Huayoung Gate was from Mount Hua.

speaker: narrator
text: Heo Do Jinin looked at Hyun Jong and smiled.

speaker: narrator
text: But Hyun Jong felt like this smile was from a viper.

speaker: hyun jong
text: “You know the Huayoung Gate?”

speaker: hyun jong
text: “How is the Wudang Sect able to know such a place? Come on, speak up, Sect Leader.”

speaker: hyun jong
text: “We need to act now.”

speaker: narrator
text: Hearing this, Hyun Jong was sweating bullets trying to think about how to deal with all this,

speaker: hyun jong
text: “Is there a problem?”

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Gunak, who noticed this, spoke up and said to the other sect leaders,

speaker: sect leader
text: “Right. Doesn’t that mean it is forbidden? Would we even care about gambling if it wasn’t in Shaolin?”

speaker: sect leader
text: “You do not understand, it means that the Shaolin will be the one handling the situation here.”

speaker: narrator
text: At that, everyone shut their mouths.

speaker: sect leader
text: “I understand you were worried, but please be careful with your words and actions. Each and every rebuke could be considered a criticism of Shaolin. If one has common sense, who would dare open such a stall without Shaolin’s permission?”

speaker: narrator
text: At those words, and in unison, all looked at the Abbot.

speaker: narrator
text: The Abbot, who looked at their expressions, smiled,

speaker: sect leader
text: “Isn’t that alright?”

speaker: sect leader
text: “But… this is the sacred ground of Shaolin…”

speaker: narrator
text: He was a bit flustered with so many gazes on him, but the abbot smiled,

speaker: sect leader
text: “I never thoUght of Shaolin as a sacred place.”

speaker: narrator
text: The Abbot lightly joined his hands together..

speaker: sect leader
text: “It is impossible to say that Shaolin is a common temple like any other in the world. Having said that, all we have is a lot of people who believe in this. Is it such a great place if so many gather, but are unable to enjoy themselves?”

speaker: narrator
text: Seeing this, the sect leaders all coUghed and agreed, and the Abbot smiled,

speaker: sect leader
text: “Isn’t it enoUgh that I am strict with my disciples? It is impossible for us to scold other sect’s disciples who came to watch the competition.”

speaker: narrator
text: As soon as those words fell, words of praise poured out,

speaker: sect leader
text: “As expected of the Abbot.”

speaker: sect leader
text: “Huhu.Right, this should be a place that is enjoyed to the fullest.”

speaker: narrator
text: It didn’t matter if the logic was right.

speaker: narrator
text: If the Abbot of the Shaolin Sect says so, it is irrefutable.

speaker: narrator
text: All the people gathered here were sect leaders, leading some of the largest sects of the world, yet no one dared speak back to this man.

speaker: narrator
text: That was the strength that Shaolin had built.

speaker: narrator
text: Hyun Jong normally would have felt thrilled at what he saw, but now he could only sigh in relief as this matter had been resolved.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘It feels like my lifespan has been reduced by ten years.’ After regaining his youth, which seemingly had extended his life, this buffer between death seemed to be eroded away with each day.

speaker: narrator
text: Hyun Jong secretly sighed, and looked at the disciples of Mount Hua with sharp eyes.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘It is fine to lose my life, but you need to get good results in this competition unconditionally!’ Their name had already been blown away, so get the right results!

speaker: narrator
text: At least the results!

speaker: narrator
text: You damned idiots!

speaker: hyun jong
text: “Does it seem like Sect Leader is having a hard time?”

speaker: narrator
text: At Baek Cheon’s words, Chung Myung looked away, not being able to understand,

speaker: chung myung
text: “The gambling one. That…”

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung put his hand in his sleeve and pulled something out.

speaker: chung myung
text: “… well, how did you bring that money?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Money is meant to be invested. Look, bringing it has its uses.”

speaker: narrator
text: It gave him the momentum to sweep away the gambling board.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon shook his head looking at Chung Myung’s bright smile.

speaker: narrator
text: But instead of scolding him for it, he looked at Chung Myung with warm eyes.

speaker: chung myung
text: “What is with those eyes? Suddenly I hate him… but Sasuk.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “I am leaving everything to sasuk in the first round. You know what will happen if you lose.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “I need to make some money, so make sure to move according to how I bet. If you don’t want to use a pickaxe in the coal mine, you’d better win even if it means death.”

speaker: narrator
text: Such warm words from sajil to sasuk.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Can I ask you one thing?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “When did I say no? Feel free to ask?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Who do you think will win?”

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung looked at him with a pathetic gaze.

speaker: narrator
text: Then, Baek Cheon smiled and said,

speaker: chung myung
text: “Who is most likely to be a winner except you?”

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung thoUght about it.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung lightly touched his chin,

speaker: chung myung
text: “Of course, I know who is the strongest. But a spar isn’t necessarily won by the strongest.”

speaker: narrator
text: Just as Baek Cheon was about to ask him to explain, Chung Myung said,

speaker: chung myung
text: “But I still see those who have a chance. First, the one from Shaolin.”

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon nodded his head, That one punch.

speaker: narrator
text: That alone seemed enoUgh to put his name on the winning list.

speaker: narrator
text: No one who watched that spar would disagree.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung thoUght for a moment and said,

speaker: chung myung
text: “Ah, and the Namgung family too?”

speaker: narrator
text: The mention of Namgung’s Do Wei made Baek Cheon frown.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “There is one from Wudang and another from the Hebei Peng family. And… um, should Jin Geum-Ryong be put on that list too? Some will have a hard time dealing with him.”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “… Namgung, Wudang, and Peng.”

speaker: narrator
text: All of them had made names for themselves.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Aside from those three?”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Is this another ‘except for them’ question?”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Without me, it would be sasuk who reigns, isn’t that natural?”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “What? Not confident?”

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon bit his lips which were twitching,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Mount Hua’s Baek Cheon!”

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon got up from his seat and looked back at Chung Myung,

speaker: chung myung
text: “Wait, I will prove it and come back.”

speaker: narrator
text: And he walked to the stage in the most beautiful form.

speaker: narrator
text: There was no better picture of Baek Cheon wearing his black robe and walking with broad shoulders.

speaker: narrator
text: Seeing that beautiful figure, Chung Myung smiled, ‘Such a simple mind.’ Of course, Baek Cheon was one of the hundred contenders.

speaker: narrator
text: And if he looked at the probabilities, there were three likely outcomes.

speaker: narrator
text: But winning would be difficult.

speaker: narrator
text: Because there was one monster.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung’s eyes fell on where Shaolin’s disciples were sitting.

speaker: narrator
text: He was a guy who could not be defeated.

speaker: narrator
text: Current Baek Cheon would have trouble dealing with him.

speaker: narrator
text: Of course, it wasn’t like there was no way to win.

speaker: narrator
text: But as he had mentioned previously, when talking about skill, it wasn’t a dragged-out conversation.

speaker: narrator
text: Maybe in a few years, Baek Cheon could grow exponentially in comparison to now.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung scratched his head,

speaker: chung myung
text: “I didn’t talk about the other one from the Southern Edge Sect.”

speaker: narrator
text: Well, it would be fine.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon, who went to the stage, looked at his opponent and smiled, ‘Clearly, his name was Jong Seohan.’ A second-class disciple.

speaker: narrator
text: He was someone who was relegated to the sidelines and left in the shadow of Jin Geum-Ryong.

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan looked at Baek Cheon and smiled,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Thus, I ended up meeting the Handsome White Dragon.”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Have you come with a prepared mind?”

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon looked at him and looked at where the disciples of Southern Edge were gathered.

speaker: narrator
text: Jin Geum-Ryong was staring at him with cold eyes,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Where are you looking?”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “You’ve got a vain reputation and it seems like you are fine. You don’t deserve to be called sahyung thoUgh. You haven’t forgotten, right? Your disciples of Mount Hua have been below us. You are all reaping your luck next to Mount Hua’s Divine Dragon.”

speaker: narrator
text: But Baek Cheon was a calm person,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Ah, right. I agree.”

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan looked at Baek Cheon.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Are you saying that you have no pride?’ How could you be calm after hearing such words?

speaker: narrator
text: But Baek Cheon continued to speak in a calm manner,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “The second-class disciples of Mount Hua never defeated the Southern Edge Sect, and my reputation is clearly overstated.”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “But why does it matter?”

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon drew his sword,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “If I haven’t won before, I just need to win from now on, and if I have a reputation, I just need to make sure that it doesn’t get broUght down. I am sorry, but the disciples of Mount Hua aren’t the same as before.”

speaker: narrator
text: Seeing Jong Seohan angry, Baek Cheon smiled.

speaker: narrator
text: In the past, he trained all this time to defeat his brother, but looking back now, he would have never been able to defeat any disciple of Southern Edge.

speaker: narrator
text: There was a huge difference between Mount Hua and Southern Edge.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Ah. One correction.”

speaker: narrator
text: At Baek Cheon’s words, Jong Seohan’s eyes looked doubtful,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Mount Hua doesn’t cling to the past, but I guess I cling to the past as I am a little timid. The memories of me being smashed in the past are still clear to me.”

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon tapped his temple with his finger, and Jong Seohan frowned and said,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Don’t worry. I will make new memories for you. The experience of being smashed again in front of so many people will not be easy, so it will remain with you for the rest of your life.”

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon smiled brightly,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “As you said, it was Chung Myung who defeated Southern Edge and not us. But now we have a chance, and if I beat you, Jin Geum-Ryong will be next.”

speaker: narrator
text: And looking at Jong Seohan’s bright blue eyes,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “I will completely cut the ties Southern Edge has with Mount Hua with my own hands. Watch it. I will prove that the Southern Edge Sect is no longer an opponent for Mount Hua.”

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon took the stance of the 24 Movement Plum Blossom Sword technique and raised his sword.

speaker: narrator
text: The unique black and pure white qi.

speaker: narrator
text: A sword that shone in the sunlight.

speaker: narrator
text: Everything looked outstanding.

speaker: narrator
text: He had the appearance of a hero from a story.

speaker: narrator
text: All the people were mesmerized by it.

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan couldn’t look at him like that,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Let’s see if that sword is as sharp as your mouth!”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Don’t worry, it is sharp.”

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan, who was pushed back with words, grunted and drew his sword.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon turned his head and looked at his brother.

speaker: narrator
text: Jin Geum-Ryong had a cold look as usual.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Take a good look, hyung.’ The Southern Edge would fall from the hands of Baek Cheon and not Chung Myung.

speaker: narrator
text: This would be the last piece of consideration he would show to his family.

speaker: narrator
text: Looking at the stage, Yoon Jong clenched his fists.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Chung Myung, sasuk will win right?”

speaker: narrator
text: When Yoon Jong turned, Chung Myung’s chair was empty, and only Jo Gul stood there next to him with a shaky expression,

speaker: chung myung
text: “… he went to collect money.”

speaker: narrator
text: This sect was walking backward.

speaker: narrator
